lines.loc: The State Council of Ursus Autumn, 1099

lines.x: Could someone please tell me how much longer His Imperial Majesty will allow this charade to continue? A day? A month? Or perhaps a whole year?
I've quite frankly had enough already. The Victorians have gained the power to control Catastrophes, and even those devil savages have built battleships that fly in the sky.
And yet we are supposed to waste our time discussing how to deal with this jester?

Sir, if I may... This is a kindness bestowed upon us by His Imperial Majesty... After all, many citizens continue to believe the madman still possesses some form of noble virtue, and that is why His Imperial Majesty was willing to allow the judgment to be publicized.
The limit was seven days, and as of moments earlier... it has been six days and twenty-one hours.
Nonsense... Absolute nonsense! Are we supposed to sit here and endure these obscenities, hoping the madman will come to his senses at the toll of the bell on the final day?
Sorry, sir, but I am afraid so. The good news is once the bell tolls, the State Council will begin voting here and now.
And when the time comes, you will never have to endure this ever again.
Hmph... I know that, of course.
Alright, now get out. The clown's death may be a foregone conclusion, but until he is in the ground or burned to ash...
...he will still be Ursus's last Yurodstvo.
Huh? Did my eyes deceive me? That looked like an Imperial Guard uniform...
Is he back already? The break didn't last five minutes... He didn't even have a sip of water during his speech!
The final speech—
All eyes are focused on an out-of-place, ragged man walking to the center of the council hall in big strides.
He is standing tall, yet his limbs seem relaxed. There is no hint of worry for the future or signs of pain that come with the struggle for survival on his face.
Barely five minutes ago, he made a speech here about love, compassion, and how he forgives all those who decided his death.
His six-day-long speech has fatigued his audience, yet he appears completely unaffected. He raises his hand, and all around him immediately fall silent.
He begins to speak with a smile.
My dear friends, or perhaps those who regard me as an enemy. Surely, all of us know that this is the end.
As such, I shall not be discussing the meaning of love, or proclaiming my forgiveness to you for all the mistakes you have made, and all that you put me through.
What I shall now discuss... is the future. The future of a blind, illiterate man.
Preposterous...
We must keep in mind that recounting history is a most noble power, and such power can easily blind us.
For two decades, His Imperial Majesty has shared his glory with his subjects, yet they have never made the necessary preparations to accept his blessings.
I have never once doubted the Tsar's kindness. However, a kindness that is out of time may as well be poison.
No doubt the rebellious Armies and the Northern Tundra Infected have proven my point.

The despicable villains raised their rebellious heads and spilled their vile blood on these holy lands. The arrogant scholars proclaim that they have mastered the truth, yet their harmful words feed on the souls of the innocent like wicked worms.

This ignorant arrogance propagated like wildfire. For this, the masses have forgotten what it means to be humble. The Empire has forgotten what it means to be kind. Now, our grand empire and her citizens find themselves alienated from one another.
I can see the past and the future. Not because I am wise; I am only a fool. Rather, the trajectory of the future is already written in the trails of the past.
I do not regret revealing to the masses the future that I saw. Even if I must stand trial for this, I have no choice but to spread this prophecy.
I saw the end of Ursus. I saw the fall of a great empire.
And that is why I am standing here today. I ask His Imperial Majesty the Tsar to prevent all this. If my worthless life is the price to accomplish this, then please, by all means.
If even more blood needs to be spilled to cleanse Ursus of its filth, then I beg His Imperial Majesty to spill it. For an even greater cause, and for Ursus's eternal glory.
Once this trial is over, no matter the sentence, I shall begin my sacred journey.
Again and again, I shall extol the virtues of humility in an attempt to restore our unity and the Empire's glory.
Perhaps you think me an arrogant man overstepping my place, but I lead no armies and make no laws.
Perhaps you think I am fatally ill, but I am not a madman, nor am I out of my mind.

My hands are emptier than even those of the junkmen. My clothes are more tattered than even those of the Infected. All I've done—my mistake—is to see a different history and to hear different voices than the others.
The traditions that drive us never to whitewash our pain, long buried deep underground, will again be unearthed.
The cries that compel us never to attempt diminishing this cruelty, long fallen silent, will again ring loud and clear—
When that time finally arrives, perhaps... perhaps our great nation's future will change for the better. All angers will be quelled, all sins will be forgiven, and all pain...
All of it will be assuaged by the purity of patience.

lines.loc: Northern Tundra Wilderness, Ursus Year ■■■■

lines.x: Quick! Run!
Keep running and don't look back! Just like the others!
No, we won't get away... We can't run from this! Even if we make it out of Ursus, nothing but death awaits!
*cough*— *cough cough*!
Stop breathing so hard. You're going to breathe the snow in and choke—
Indeed.
He will choke on his blood.
*cough*—
What? How did you break free so fast? No way... Impossible—
You are a clever one, but it is not enough. The illusion you cast on me was rooted in your own experiences, to a certain extent.
Your mistake was not realizing that putting us through the pain in your illusion is like trying to shift mountains with a feather.
At the very least, this demonstrated your power. You have what is needed to become its replacement.
I told you. I don't know a thing about you or your demonic power testing, and I'm not interested.
This is not an invitation. This is an ultimatum. Of course, you have been able to save them not once, not twice... but three times before I tired of this.
But I tire of this charade now.
Stay back, unless you want to take away my corpse.
Your 'voice' is no longer calm. You are beginning to doubt yourself.
What is the price to use the Yurodstvo's power when you are not one?
I don't understand what you are getting at.
All things have a price, and you are nearing your end.
Perhaps we're both going to die together at that end.
Just us.
So you did not hear them... or perhaps you simply do not have the energy left to notice more?

You did not hear the sound of those footsteps on the snow... You are trying to save these people, even if it means sacrificing yourself to kill us... yet you are unable to hear that they are only meaningless parasites.

They are never willing to stray too far from their homeland. They claim to be unwilling to turn their backs on their motherland and hold onto the meagerest glimmer of hope. If they choose to run, they intentionally slow their steps, or even turn back to face their deaths.
By leaving this place, they will become unable to confirm their own existence, even if this existence would bring them nothing but despair.
Unfortunately, your warning is meaningless.
What—
Just as you told them.
'Do not look back.'
Ugh—!
*cough*— *cough cough*! Hah—
I don't wish to bring Ursus a Yurodstvo whose bodily functions are ruined. This would waste a lot of time. Even with your abilities, it's hardly a fair deal.
Thus, again I recommend that you give up, and come with me.
I'll say it again, too. I have no idea what a 'Yurodstvo' is, and I have no interest in becoming one!
Besides, even if I go with you, what about the Infected? The ones you chased... into a dead end...
They have their own destiny to walk.
No...
Perhaps you should 'speak' your mind more clearly.
I said, no.
I need to...
There is silence and tension all around. A complete 'sentence' has yet to surface out of the aether, and the snow continues to fall around them, but the black entity has stopped marching forward.
Before the next cluster of snow even touches the ground, a dark mist has already begun to spread.

lines.st: 'Stay.'

lines.x: He suddenly loses his urge to continue forward. The other half of his cognition, stripped of reason, waits for the sight he saw earlier to reappear.
The same trick won't work twice...
But nothing. There is nothing.
There is only the inexplicable pause, the pitch black substance endlessly emanating from him, and the thick dust accumulated from the mist.
Almost like there is black snow falling.
What...? But I—
The demon inside of me has but a slight leak, yet you are able to use my power to shackle me?

I am just using the... illusion in that filth trapped in your body to make you face yourself.
No more meaningless struggles. Else...
*cough*...

Else, you will die.
In your own Dominion...
*cough*–– *cough* *cough*!!!
Now sleep...
Bozhe moy. This... This—
Boy... Boy! Are you okay? Is that black monster still coming after us—
—Aah!!!
*cough*... *cough*...
He... won't chase you anymore.
What?
Quick. Get out of here. Head south before he comes to. And don't you turn back again.
You have to leave this place, if you want to live...
Wh-What are you talking about? He won't chase us anymore?
What's that supposed to mean?
*cough*—
Are you saying you managed to convince that unreasonable lich?
Enough! Go!
Go...? Go where?
The preacher told us before: 'Those who turn their backs on the motherland can scarcely be called human.' We... We don't want that!
We don't want to turn our backs on the Tsar, on Ursus... and you...
You managed to get him to stop chasing us... You saved us, and you accomplished the impossible, but you still won't tell us your name? Just who are you?
That's not important. I...
Or... are you perhaps—
Are you perhaps the Yurodstvo who vanished long ago?
What...?
But the Yurodstvo should be in the splendorous palaces of Deity Grypherburg, at the Tsar's side.
No...
Right. Right! If you don't belong here, then this blessing received is a stolen one. In that case, it's only fair that we're punished for it.
'We must endure,' 'we must persevere...' 'We must be silent!' But we haven't been able to do any of that...
Even so, you protected these sinful souls.
In that case, you must be the Yurodstvo. Otherwise, you wouldn't have the kindness and compassion to offer the Infected a helping hand, as equals.
In that case, there is only one name you could bear.
The name that you don't want us praising. The name shared by all Yurodstvos.

lines.st: 'Gabrielus.'

lines.x: Gabrielus. Gabrielus...
It's the Yurodstvo. Our Yurodstvo has returned!
The Yurodstvo? Like the one in the tales?
Is it the Yurodstvo that Mama said would love us even if we had the Stone Disease, even if it is not by our side?
Yes, it's her! She has returned! She defeated the villains and saved us! That's something only the Yurodstvo could do!
The Yurodstvo has saved us! Yurodstvo—
The boisterous crowd and their hands all gush toward her.

They keep pushing and pulling, like waves splashing upon a beach, all in an attempt to so much as touch the corner of their idol's shirt, even if her attire is hardly any different from the tattered clothing they are wearing.
Fully surrounded, she is no longer able to prop herself up or think. The moment her knees twitch and fall to the snow, she seems as though she is going to collapse and pass out right here and now.
But she can't. Not here. The Royal Guard is still here, and those executioners who want to use her to kill even more people are still here. She can't close her eyes. She can't... give up just yet.
*cough*... *cough*...
Ugh—
That moment, she even seems to think that her tongue is once again working with her mouth to clearly enunciate her words. However, her body simply curls up quietly and gets smaller... and smaller.
She endures it until the pain subsides.
Yurodstvo—
She is not the Yurodstvo, yet there is a price to pay.
And despite being the one mistaken for the Yurodstvo, she doesn't know what's the price she has to pay for this absurd charade. Sometimes, not knowing the price is itself a price.
Mantra? What's wrong?
The leader's quiet voice suddenly echoes in all the Rhodes Islanders' minds.
Everyone, stop.

opts: This...

lines.x: Repeat. Everyone, stop immediately.
The area ahead is demonically corrupted.
Demonic corruption? Here?
But aren't we one-third of an Ursus away from Sami? How could this possibly—
Doctor?

opts: This may not be a normal demonic corruption...
'Dominion.'

lines.loc: Central Far North Mining District, Ursus Autumn, 1101

opts: Raidian's squad's last known coordinates should be around here.

lines.x: According to the timestamp of the signal sent back to the city, they last sent out a message ten days ago.
The laboratory she mentioned is roughly three kilometers up ahead.

opts: Mon3tr, have you faced the Royal Guard before?
Mon3tr, are you able to analyze anything here?

lines.x: I'm looking.
Based on the scale and shape of the corruption, this is very likely to be caused by 'Dominion.'
Right, a fight against the Royal Guard probably took place here.
It's strange, though. For such an intense battle, there aren't any traces of Arts nearby.
I understand you are worried, Doctor.
But knowing Raidian, she would definitely have tried to leave tracks we could decipher.

opts: At the very least, this explains something happened here that called for the Royal Guard's involvement.
Clearly that the Grand Duchess is hiding something from us.
Raidian's squad might be in an extremely dangerous predicament.
Raidian's squad might have been involved in whatever happened here.

lines.x: Correct.
Whether they were going after Raidian or the laboratory, it seems we need to pick up the pace.

opts: We need to find a safe route to the research institute.
Elysium?

lines.loc: Central Far North Mining District, Ursus

lines.x: Looks like my equipment's not working right, it can't detect the precise range of the contamination.
I think this is the miners' accommodation. The real danger here might be whoever's still around, really. This is demonic contamination we're talking about.
Captain says there's no trace of any mental activity in the entire mine, human or animal.
At the very least, the contamination hasn't blocked the way to the institute. It's mostly headed south, actually.
We can swing a detour around the north. It'll take us roughly three hours longer than the original plan, but the time loss will be manageable.
(Nods)
Wow...
I've been thinking this for a while now, but you two work really well together!
Elysium and Mantra have been working together for many years now.
Hahaha... You could say I'm Rhodes Island's most experienced operator when it comes to working with the captain.

It takes a lot out of her to 'speak', so if it's not an emergency, I get to be the one passing on the message.
So, what do you think, Doctor? If we set off now, we should make it before sunset—
Not that the sun sets in the Ursine Far North this time of year...

opts: We set off right now.
Get our friend up to speed on our detour.

lines.x: Don't fall behind now, Eloysius.
Sorry, was just inspecting the mine equipment, nothing to do with our travels together.
What's going on? Why did you stop for so long?
According to the map, we're very close to the institute. Don't tell me you've found some—
...thing?
It's... traces of Dominion.
The Royal Guard was here.
You don't seem shocked?
I hope you're not insinuating I'm hiding something, Gospozha Amiya, or I'd be very hurt.
I hope you share any pertinent information with us, at least while we are collaborating.
What do you know about the whereabouts of the Royal Guard?
First of all, only the Tsar himself has the authority to know the comings and goings of the Royal Guard, let alone a lowly sergeant such as myself.
But honestly speaking, after the illustrious Grand Duchess of the Far North declared martial law and shut down the mines, the Royal Guard showing up would not be much of a shock.
Then, if they're already conducting an investigation of their own here, where does that leave you?
You're a clever one, aren't you?
The news of abnormal Infected miners in Central Far North has made waves, and many are curious.
Naturally, while most Ursine have a right to curiosity, they do not have the same right to information.
Please understand that, as a sergeant, I am limited in what I am allowed to say. I hope my explanation will not cause too much confusion, Gospozha Amiya.
No, it's fine. I understand the position you are in.
I've never minced words about how this job could improve the résumé of an Imperial Guardswoman like me.
Though I did imagine it being much easier...
Anyhow, that dream is dead. The Royal Guard being involved is a bad omen, no matter how you look at it.
If that's the case, are you still set on traveling with us?
Of course. I chose to take on this task, and quitting halfway would hardly be the decent thing to do.
Before we continue onwards, shall we reaffirm our trust in one another?
I look forward to our continued cooperation.
Just like the time we met...

lines.loc: Bresk, Far North, UrsusThree days ago

opts: Elysium! Give us some direction!

lines.x: Mierda... The signal's all laggy, it's like there's something interfering with our encrypted frequency!
Whatever, not important... Ten guardsmen to your west, turn right at the next crossing!
What are you talking about?! The west is on our right!
Did I say right? Gah—
Do you see the city hall? Go in the opposite direction!
I see it, but the crossing ahead is where the plates are disconnected—
What?!
There's nothing like that on this stupid map!
Well, you're practically surrounded by guards, so that's your only way out!
Got it.
Can you keep up, Amiya?
I can!
Hold on tight, Doctor!

opts: Doing my best here—

lines.x: Your best isn't good enough. Fine, I'll hold onto you instead!
Phew...
You alright, Doctor?

opts: I'm good.
I've gotten pretty used to it by now.
Next time, I can try running by myself.

lines.x: Haha, glad you're doing well! Guess I can relax, then!
We've arrived at the designated location, Elysium.
Elysium?
...Don't...*bzzt*...stop...oving...*bzzt*—
...There's...*bzzt* *bzzt*...uards...
The line cut out.
The terminal says Elysium's still moving, but we don't have any way to reestablish communication.
It's like short-range comms have been unstable ever since we entered Bresk.
There's also no way of telling if it's because of the Far North's extreme weather, or human interference.

opts: I am curious, though...
Why would Bresk send so many guards to detain 'inter-city merchants'?

lines.x: It is rather strange...
There haven't been any issues with the ID cards Closure forged for us.

opts: Maybe we're not the ones they're watching out for.
Maybe they're watching us for different reasons.
But putting that aside...
Bresk is supposed to be a quiet city.
It's strange that it's so fortified.

lines.x: This isn't anything like the intelligence we had... If this goes on, we won't make it into the mining district.
Doctor, shouldn't we rendezvous with Mantra's team and discuss—
Doctor! Finally got you!
Keep moving! There are guards heading your way!

opts: I think I figured that out, thanks...

lines.x: They're fast—
Get behind me, Amiya!
No, don't engage!
Alright, interloping scum, hands up!

opts: Let's take a deep breath here.
What laws have we violated to cause such a fuss?

lines.x: You trespassed in the industrial zone, and attempted to infiltrate a private factory, violating Bresk's migration regulations and Ursine urban public security measures.
Could I ask you to reconfirm, sir? When we entered the city, I'd never heard anything about a prohibition on foreigners entering the industrial zone—
It's a new regulation, went into effect just after you reached Bresk.
You will be detained under the law, and then Public Security will decide if you are to be deported or exiled, considering your circumstances.
If we inadvertently violated Bresk's new regulation, then I deeply apologize.
We're just here to look for an inter-city merchant. It was stated clearly in the itinerary declaration we submitted upon entry to the city.
Save it for Public Security.

opts: We didn't mean to offend.
There may be a way for both of us to save time here.
I believe Bresk, Pearl of the Far North, treats its guests well.
I'm sure the Grand Duchess is as kind as the rumors say.

lines.x: Enough of that, I don't give a damn. I'm just doing my job.
Sir...
This way.
No, sir. We can't delay any longer.
I'm sorry.
You see Amiya shift her body, surreptitiously protecting you. Mon3tr observes your surroundings, looking for a way out.
You don't know how things work around here.
Get them.
(Warning roar)
Err... Ah!
What are you standing around for?! Get in there already!
What, can't even handle a little Feline girl?
S...Sir, look behind them.
That looks like th-th-the—
Spit it out already!
—The Imperial Guard!
Yerunda! Those pretentious bastards should be in Grypherburg, what would they be doing in Bresk?
B-But those clothes—
I am terribly sorry, sir, but I'm afraid she is right this time.
You...
Eloysius Gorchekova, Sergeant of the Imperial Guard, Vanguard Battalion, here from Deity Grypherburg.
What are you playing at?
I have a simple request for you. Overlook this little breach of protocol and release our pitiful guests.
You're with them?
Unfortunately, they are indeed my friends. Since you are now aware of my history with them, surely you'll do me this favor, yes?
We're a long way from Grypherburg, why should I care about that?
Because I stopped by Public Security and filled out all the paperwork?
The young Ursus waves some papers in her hand. In the dusky gloom of polar night, none can clearly see what is written on them.
But—
If I were you, sir, I wouldn't play around with my future like that.
There are many lost travelers, but rarely a chance to curry favor with the Imperial Guard—Ah, yes, may I ask you your name, or your service number?
Alright, Sergeant Gorchekova. I'll report all this to Public Security. You may go.
May your authority be as eternally glorious as the Tsar himself.
Thank you for the kind words, sir.
But none has as eternal a glory as His Imperial Majesty. The rest of us will have to leave the stage when the time comes.
Well, I'll be off with our guests for now.
Thank you for helping us get out of that sticky situation, ma'am... but, why exactly did you—
Shh, they're still close by.
Also, my cute little Cautus, you may call me Eloysius.
'Ma'am' is too formal, while 'Guardswoman' or 'girl' is too crude. I believe names are what's best for our current relationship.

opts: Thank you for the help, Eloysius.
You really know how to handle those guards.

lines.x: They most likely thought you were ordinary traders, easy to shake down for a quick chervonets or two, same way they treat Columbian sightseers.
But you're hardly tourists or traders, now, are you?

opts: Let's cut to the chase.
Why are you helping us?

lines.x: Good, good, I appreciate candor. I will answer your questions, but not here.
Once you have gathered your friends, we can go somewhere far from prying eyes and ears.

opts: We don't have much time.

lines.x: Relax, I won't be wasting your time. This is all in service of your goal.
Well, here we are.
Where is here?
An abandoned apartment. As far as I know, the guards don't care for these nigh-empty old districts.

An empty plate may sound wasteful, but it is true. No matter how the Deity Grypherburg Times tries to cover it up, it is a widely-known fact that the population of the Far Northern cities has been dwindling these last few years.
As time goes by, this place becomes deserted, and the plate will be discarded, left to rust in the wilderness like all the other abandoned plates.
Eloysius, we need to know exactly why you came looking for us. Now, ideally.
(Vigilant stare)
Could I ask everyone to lighten up a little, please?
There are a lot of you and only one of me. What kind of threat could I possibly pose to people of your caliber?
Could I ask the Phidia lady standing outside the door to tell your friends that I'm not lying?
Captain says there's nobody else here. The district is safe.

opts: Choosing this place to talk means you also need to be covert.
I had hoped the Imperial Guard would at least treat us to dinner.

lines.x: A friend of mine used to live here. She had hoped to achieve her dream of completing a saga for the Ursine people, but was defeated by the frigid cold—She is doing very well in Grypherburg, I should add.
But thanks to her, we have this place to shelter both from the cold wind, and from people snooping around.
I'm sure I'm not the only one here who wants to stay out of trouble.
You're a funny one, my hooded friend. It takes a clever mind to grasp humor.
Once we have discussed our important business, I would be happy to entertain you with a meal if you are all still interested.
Firstly, since I've already introduced myself, may I have the pleasure of knowing your names?
We've come from Victoria and—
Still insisting that you went into the industrial zone to meet with an inter-city merchant, a widow who goes by the name of Olga?
A very reliable cover story, one that will fool a vast majority of the city's port officials.
But no matter how you put it, it is rare for a commercial party in a nomadic city to be using military-grade encrypted communications.
Was it you intercepting our communications?
No, no, I would never do something so crude.
It may have been the aurora, or the recently upgraded defense equipment. You truly are unlucky, coming to Bresk during the most interesting times it has ever seen.
I have been honest on my part. If you still have things you wish to keep hidden, then I am afraid we have to break things off here.

opts: We're a pharmaceutical company.
Rhodes Island.

lines.x: See? Honesty isn't so painful, is it?
Rhodes Island... I have heard this name mentioned many times while investigating Infected rumors from all across the land.
Based on what I have pieced together from these tales, you lot are the stuff of legends.
But no matter how true they are, the most recent instance I have encountered your name was in that paper you published about aberrant changes in the Infected.

opts: You don't strike me as a colleague in the field.
I never thought you'd be interested in Oripathy studies.

lines.x: Pardon me for letting you down, but not every newly-minted Grypherburg noble is an uneducated lout.

There is not a nation on this land that can overlook the Infected question, though their policies in answering it may differ. Anyone with a modicum of common sense would know just how important this scientific achievement of yours is.
But there is one thing I am curious about. Despite this groundbreaking discovery, Rhodes Island has kept a low profile, instead of using it to do more business.

opts: Is that why you came to find us?
This doesn't explain how you knew our whereabouts.

lines.x: Oh, that can easily be explained. I think anyone who goes into the Far North's Central Mining District right now is doing it for the same reason.
You mean you also want in?
What, do you not?
We... need to get in.
Sure enough.
You don't have a way into the mining district, and as it turns out, I have need of some Oripathy specialists. The perfect recipe for cooperation.

opts: So you want us to go into the district with you?

lines.x: Of course.
The authorities here will not look kindly on some Grypherburger sticking her nose into Far Northern affairs, after all.
As things are, your cover as merchants will do you no good, nor will it help me, so let's forget all the masks and red tape and shake hands right here. That is one solution to all our problems.
But what is it you want to do in the district? And what do you need from us?
Was I not clear?
I'm hoping Rhodes Island will accompany me into the mining district to collaborate on an investigation into the Infected affair.
The 'Infected affair'? What exactly are you referring to...?
Oh, my dear, sweet, naive little Cautus, my patience has its limits, you know. There is no time to be playing dumb.
No, that's a serious question.
We lost contact with one of our mobile medical teams in the Far North's Central Mining District, and we need to find them.
We really have no idea what this 'Infected affair' is, and I hope you will elaborate.

You want into the district just for—
Wait, so you really don't know?
That's impossible. To my knowledge, you've built many more medical posts all across Ursus in the past few years—
Simple doubt replaces the suspicion on the young woman's face, before finally shifting to a sudden realization.
She understands how her assumptions have led her astray.
—No. You just said 'mobile medical team'...

No fixed offices means no steady access to a network of contacts with the latest intelligence. I don't know what issues Rhodes Island is currently facing, but it seems the information you have is limited.
And because this is Ursus, you have been unable to do much. Because of your lack of information, you have been blindly running into walls this whole time... Haven't you?
Chyort poberi! I was too impatient.

opts: We really don't know what this 'Infected affair' is.
Does it have to do with Bresk's current state of affairs?

lines.x: Forget it. Forget I said anything. That favor before was free of charge, no need to thank me.
Wait!
The very fact that you came looking for us means there is the possibility we can work together on this.
Could you not tell us what exactly happened in the mining district?
I really... Gah! I really should have thought things through more before making a choice.
And here I was, thinking you were racking your brains on how to enter the district upon hearing the news. You should know that this information is worth quite a lot on the black market.
We're not that kind of organization... but that doesn't matter right now.
We have a natural reason to be invested in anything to do with the Infected. That's why you came looking for us, wasn't it?
Please, tell us.
Fine, fine! Since I'm already here, I may as well do something, even if it is just for some Infected.
The Infected are Ursine citizens, too.
You are correct, precious little Cautus. The Infected are indeed Ursines, and that is before we mention those miners that the Tsar personally promised 'life, not survival'.
The miners are the backbone of Ursus, keeping its war machines running. A fact that those corrupt old nobles and their puppets haven't realized yet.
But even they must know that Ursus must change.
My word, what am I even babbling about...? Good thing those sharp-tongued, poison-spitting colleagues of mine aren't here, or they would laugh me into my grave.
You should not feel ashamed for this.
Spoken words are like spilled cystymilk: they freeze solid in this weather.
So what is this 'Infected affair'? It sounds like it has to do with the Infected miners?
Listen, if you dare leak any of this...
You have my word that no matter what you have to say, we will not disclose it.
There is no benefit to making an enemy out of Ursus. We know that well.
Give me a moment, I need to figure out where to start...
You know, of course, that Bresk borders the Far North's Central Mining District, and is also the largest nomadic city in the north of Ursus.
This means the city administrates a total of thirty thousand square kilometers' worth of mines scattered across the Far North, alongside two hundred thousand Infected.

opts: We are familiar with the basic facts of this place.
What issues arise in this sort of system?

lines.x: Please don't look at me like that... Sorry, I knew this sort of talk would anger you.
But the Tsar does not wish for these Infected miners to be treated as cheap labor. On the contrary, his decrees have shown a lot of care for the people of Bresk.
Things have been going rather smoothly in the city these past few years, until three months ago...
Yes, this is where your company's area of expertise comes in, along with that paper.
Three months ago, large numbers of 'abnormal Infected' were discovered in the Central Mining District. Their symptoms were very similar to the ones described in your paper.
(Three months ago? Wasn't that...?)

opts: You mean...?
That's the reason why Bresk is under martial law?
How many 'abnormal' Infected are there in the district?

lines.x: That's the question.
Right now, nobody knows just how many 'normal' Infected are left in the Central Mining District.

lines.loc: Meeting Room, New Rhodes Island

lines.x: Any clues on the Sarcophagus?
The very latest activity we know of involving the Sarcophagus came after the Chernobog-Lungmen incident, when the Ursine government retrieved the entire system.
As for what they actually plan to do with it, they've been keeping it under tighter wraps than ever. There's nothing more about it in Kal'tsit's records.

opts: It's not like we can directly ask the Ursine government to borrow it...
Do we have any other way of finding its location?

lines.x: Looking at the official register made after the Chernobog research institute was destroyed, there were no survivors other than Sergei.
We did manage to find some clues when looking into what happened to him afterwards.
He had established a new institute to continue researching the Sarcophagus. In the short period of time that the institute was operating, one name really stood out to us—
Artyom Chaadayev.
He was vice-director at Sergei's institute, but he's a complete ghost, with no verifiable academic or political background to speak of.
We may be able to make a rational deduction...

opts: That this man had a special connection to Sergei?
That this man had a special connection to the institute?

lines.x: No matter what his connection may be, he might be the key to our breakthrough.
So we need to find this Artyom?
No, things get a little more complicated from here on out...
He's proven to be very hard to track ever since Sergei's institute shut down.
According to Kal'tsit's intelligence, he died of heart failure in the Grypherburg Imperial Sanatorium last winter.

opts: He maintained a good relationship with Ursus to his last.
Maybe we can look into the people who knew him.

lines.x: Great idea! That's what I was thinking too.
There's another researcher who had a connection to Artyom.
Ksenia Markovna Nelidova.
We have a lot more detail on her background. Before the Sarcophagus incident, she had briefly served as Ilia's assistant at the Chernobog research institute, and had crossed paths with Kal'tsit.
Recent intelligence indicates she is currently in the Ursine Far North, working at the Central Mining District Laboratory, administered by the city of Bresk.
That's where Artyom's final letters were sent.

opts: It doesn't sound like a particularly reliable clue...
But it's the only clue we've got.
We should go to the research institute and find her.

lines.x: I have to remind you, Doctor.
While we're all determined to bring Kal'tsit back, we must thoroughly evaluate the risks of every mission.
Rhodes Island cannot bear any more losses.

opts: But...

lines.x: I'll go.
Raidian?
Ursus has always been a dangerous place, and we have very limited intelligence for this mission.
And there are many important decisions the Doctor and Amiya must make while Rhodes Island is being rebuilt.
Raidian, I can—
I know you're a capable young woman now, Amiya, but that is exactly why you need to stay here and handle our most important tasks.
The latest news from Laterano is also worrying, and the Doctor will need to see to it personally. Allowing Rhodes Island to steady its footing in our new home is our top priority.
You're right...
If it were just an infiltration mission, then Misery's team would be the best choice, but information gathering is my team's specialty.
Besides, there's no one better to send back to Ursus than me, is there?
You make some real compelling arguments, Raidian...
But it's your decision, Doctor.

opts: This will be a very dangerous mission.
Stay safe, Raidian.
Rhodes Island cannot lose anyone else.

lines.x: We will. We'll act with caution as a mobile medical team.
The rest of you, take care of yourselves while we're out, okay?
Of course...
I hope that all of you come home safe.
'...I shall meditate and share in his pain.'
'One man's pain is everyone's pain, one man's sin is everyone's sin...'
'May sinners repent with all their heart, may their sins be forgiven, and their souls find salvation...'

lines.loc: Central Far North Mining District, UrsusFive days ago

lines.x: Wargh—!
Ahhhhhh—!
Oh my...
It's... okay, ba-babushka. D-Don't... be scared.
It's U-Uchitel Rankin... He's chased away the P-Patrol Unit chasing us.
We're s-safe for now.
Yes... for now.
Where else can we go? Where must we go to find shelter...?
B-Babushka... are-are you hungry...? I'll go ask Hawley... for another piece of bread.
I'm fine... You're such a sweet child, but I really am.
Go see Rankin.
He has done so much, his spirit must be nearly worn away.
Go see him. Do not let him be burdened any more than he already is.
Uchitel, he...
Phew...
Under the twilight sky, the young man's stern countenance eases.
The cold soon cools the warm blood staining his pickaxe. The enemy before him is completely routed.
Don't... Don't kill me! You're not allowed to do anything to me, I surrendered!
But we haven't accepted that surrender.
Tell me, how many of these squads are pursuing us?
Chyort! I don't—I don't know!
Every Patrol Unit's independent, we don't talk to each other!
Tell me something I don't know, or I'll have no reason to let you live.
Wait, don't! Listen to me!
The chips you have in you! The diagnostic chips!
Chips? You mean the ones used to check how infected we are?
Ha... Hahaha...
'Rankin the Noble', 'Rankin the Hero', are you really so naive?
Those chips send your coordinates to the mine's research facility and nearby guard posts. The moment you try and leave the mining district, or the chip leaves your body—
Boom!
Losing a single leg would be lucky. Losing both is definitely possible. You won't be running anywhere like that, haha!
Just give up already, Rankin... You think you can leave this mine by killing a Patrol Unit?
There's nowhere to go. Ursus will chase you forever.
Surrender. It's the only way you won't die a miserable death.
Don't listen to him, Rankin! If the chips really did blow up, we'd have a bunch of three-legged burdenbeasts walking around!
The Patrol Units are worse than bastards! An honest one of them is like an aphid in winter! I got to know them before I became infected!
Gah—!
You've done enough today, Rankin. Leave this one to me!
N-No!
St-Stop—killing people!
We don't... need to! N-Nobody's... badly hurt...
Uchitel!
Let him go.
Get back, Vetochki! What's the point in showing a man like him mercy?
It was these bastards who killed Paulina, all because she was caring for an unregistered child... She was pregnant when they hanged her!
Have you forgotten all the terrible things they've done?
Urgh! *cough* *cough*
Haha, thank you, Vetochki.
I remember you... You were the meekest of all the children. You will have a happy ending, even if the rest of these scum—
Uchitel!
...At least I didn't let him bleed out slowly in the snow.
Stand up, Vetochki. Go to the back and find that doctor for me.
Paul, go get Nikto and tell him to gather everyone who wants to leave the mining district at the south dormitory.
Get them ready, we need these chips out of us.
You believe what that guy said?
Doesn't matter, we need the chips out of our bodies. Even if they don't explode, they definitely transmit our location.
The Patrol Units have already found us once, and we don't know when the next squad will show up. If this keeps on, we'll be on the back foot the entire time.
Alright, I'll get it done.
Uchitel, y-you pro-promised... me...
Oh, you're still here.
I didn't promise you anything, Vetochki.
Besides, if the rest of the bastards in his units are dead, why should he be any exception? Because of his arrogance? Because of his ridicule?
No...
...Never mind. You wouldn't understand. Forget what I just said.
Right, take this.
What... is it?
A stopwatch. I found it in the institute, should still work. When we get a minute, I'll teach you how to use it.
From now on, you won't need to live your life counting meaningless numbers, worrying about a miscount leaving people dead in the mines.
At least now... Go, Vetochki. Go help me find her.
Uchitel, your hand... is shaking...
Hush. Go do what you have to do.
...At least... let me...
Pray that your... soul... can be... saved.
Even though you've seen how those men treated us, and seen how I killed them?
Y-Yes.
Do not be so quick to bless others, or to forgive them, Vetochki. Me included.
Now go already. We don't have time.
...Gospodin Rankin.
Hello, Gospozha Serafina.
Though we have not known each other for long, I want to believe that, since your medical team was willing to come to such a remote place to treat the Infected, you are people I can trust.
I am sorry that I now have no choice but to ask for your help.
Please, go ahead. As long as it's something I can help with.
I just learned that inside every miner, there is a tracking chip that could explode at any moment. We need a way to extract them so we can escape the Patrol Units.
I am prepared to serve as a test subject myself.
If it is successful, please perform the surgery on the others. If it does unfortunately explode, then I hope you will be able to give me first aid.
If I die... then I ask you to tell everyone waiting for me about this.
Now, I must ask you to leave—
No. Let me do an examination first. May I?
Go ahead.
...I regret to inform you, these chips really could explode.
They contain miniaturized, high-powered Originium bombs. If the chip does not receive electrical signals from the local district relay or the human body, it will trigger an explosion...
If you hope to get everyone out of this district, then you must remove the chips.
Amazing. Is this your Arts at work?
Of course...
I may be able to temporarily copy the signal to fool the receiver. But I must warn you, this still carries a great deal of risk.
We can't afford to wait for a perfect solution. Your idea is good enough.
One moment, then.
Raidian starts using her Arts, causing beams of energy to resonate at the same frequency as the chip's receiver.
Rankin nods and sits down, grasping the hilt of his dagger. It is cold, smooth, and solid as he rubs it against the calluses of his palm.
You'd best back away from me a bit, Doctor.
That won't be happening. This is the first time I've tried it, so I need to observe it in real-time.
I can guide your hand using nerve impulses. Take care not to detonate the chip.
Incredible... It's a shame we didn't meet sooner.
I hope we will still have the chance to chat after this.
Rankin wipes his dagger with some alcohol, pointing its blade at the scar on his leg before plunging it into his flesh.
Phew...
...It worked, right?
The youthful miner nearly leaps up, forgetting the cut on his leg oozing blood.
Calm down, your wound needs dressing.
Ah, of course. Thank you, Doctor. I owe you my life.
You are truly amazing. I have never seen a physician as skilled as you. You even remind me of an Ursine soldier.
What do you mean by that?
Sorry, please do not misunderstand me. This is the highest praise I can think of.
I mean true Ursine soldiers, not those Patrol Unit brutes. The ones who drove off the invading Nightzmora and freed us from the tyrannical Hippogryphs. They were true heroes.
Oh, yes, have you read Igor Velikiy's biography?
We should prepare for surgery on the others. How many will we need to operate on?
I'm afraid it's a number that will leave you and your colleagues running ragged.
My decoy electrical signal can cover the entirety of this area, and my colleagues can coordinate the surgeries. That being the case, I reckon we will need to stay here for some time.
Time is something we are short on.
Have the medical team prioritize the elderly and children, then teach the others to perform the surgery. Hand out some scalpels. My people are not squeamish.
I would appreciate it if you did not make medical decisions for me.
Ah, no, Doctor, I did not mean to order you around.
Since the duty of leading everyone out of this mining district is mine, I need to make decisions accordingly.
Patrol Units who can show up at any time, extreme weather, lack of supplies... All things that will prove fatal.
I hope we can reach an agreement on this.
No matter what happens, we need to be wary of infection. My team will oversee the sterilization procedures.
Then let's begin.
'Act first, then discuss the future.'
Blurghhh—!
*cough* *cough*! Blergh... *cough*!
Is this your limit?
When you were eating smoked sausages back in Grypherburg, did you never wonder how they were made? Never went to see the slaughterhouses in the satellite cities?
Idi nahui! Rot in hell, Rankin! Suka blyat'!
I swallowed a bunch of snow but I still can't get the smell of blood out of my nose. Next time I have the chance, I'm dumping fresh burdenbeast blood into your soup!
Fresh burdenbeast blood is expensive. A bucket can cost more than a hundred chervontsy, we can't afford that...
It was a metaphor, dammit... I didn't know you used to be a gourmet chef, Hawley.
My son transported that sort of thing when he was a truck driver. The Grypherburg nobles believed the blood of young burdenbeasts would make them beautiful.
He told me a lot of things about the city in his letters, but the last one I received was seven years ago. I hope he's still alright...
Then I'll have to ask you to make sure Nikto doesn't mess with my dinner.
I'll make sure. Nobody is messing with anyone's dinner.
I hope this won't end up being our last meal, or I'll be cursing you to the grave, Rankin.
Whatever you say. I'm in good spirits today.
It's been so long since I've felt this sort of freedom.
We have finally broken our shackles. We are free now. What we want to do, where we want to go, and how we want to live our lives, we can decide for ourselves now.
You should save the poetry recital for when we're actually safe...
So, what now?
The plan hasn't changed.
We head south to the border with Kazimierz, find the village founded by the Infected, the one that belongs only to the Infected. There will be space for all of us there.
You're just blindly believing in this 'Reunion' without even knowing if they're the real deal...
No, I have my reasons to believe.
A few years back when I was stationed at the border, I heard a lot about them, information I could trust.

They provide shelter for the Infected, give out medicine for free, and have fended off many Patrol Units who wanted to push around people like us. I believe we will find people who share our ideals there.
That just makes me think it's some Infected delusion, or the army spinning up an excuse for a bigger share of taxes.
It makes me wonder why it took until now for a banner like this to be flown.
If... If we find out they do not exist, then it should be us who raise the flag.
Hah... And here I thought your time on the border would have taught you better.
Just imagine for a moment, if you had saved that count back then instead of that Infected, you could be a lieutenant right now, maybe even a captain.
Patrol Units like the one we ran into today would be licking your boots with a wave of your finger.
Then I should be even more grateful for the choice I made that day. Otherwise, I would not have had the chance to see this nation's true face, or learn what my life's greatest calling is.
Our people have been duped and deceived for far too long! They believe their current lives are what they deserve, forgetting what they should really have!
This is not only a conflict between the Infected and ordinary folk. If one day, all the Infected in Ursus died, they would use some other standard to decide who to cast into the mines.
Why do some have everything handed to them, while others must struggle for survival? Who do the fruits of their bitter toil go to?
But who actually gets to say what people deserve?
Maybe the way this land is is the way things should be. Even if there were another Terra, even if civilization started over from scratch, things might not turn out any different from now.
No... I don't believe that.
When you opened your eyes and actually looked at everything around us, did you not feel anger? When we feel such an instinctual rage, that means everything is wrong!
You should know that, based on what you've been through, Nikto.
Don't bring up that crap, it's ancient history...
Fine... Even if it is as you say, how far are we from the border? What other things will we run into on the way there?
I have a plan. Why do you think I asked you to gather up those chips?
They didn't blow up when we took them out of us like other chips, which means they still work as positioning devices.
You...
These things have brought us so much suffering. Why not use them to our advantage against the Patrol Units?
That's not what we agreed on when we decided to escape!
You know our condition, Rankin. How many of us can even move right now? Maybe half?
We might be able to beat one or two Patrol Units, but we can't hold off all of Ursus! The only thing causing more bloodshed would do is to trap us here even further!
If we want to live, taking this step is only a matter of time.
Every Patrol Unit lackey we kill is another spare ventilator in our hands.
Any supplies we seize can be used to leave the icefields, and allow children like Vetochki to live to see another day.
Have you forgotten what gave us the opportunity to leave this place?
Even if that thing can't be called human, we were the ones who killed it.
I don't know what you're talking about.
You've become completely unreasonable, before we've even left the mining district!
I don't want to fight over this, Nikto—
Uchitel?
And... Nikto, you're... here too...
What is it?
N-Nothing. I just came to see if... you had eaten yet...
Oh, you don't need to ask this guy. His stomach's already full of his grand ideals.
Haha... You're not really wrong.
You should focus on our physician friends, it was they who did all the hard work today.
I just went to... see them. They're... about to... rest.
Good. You've worked hard too.
How's the wound, does it hurt?
I... I'm okay. Everyone's... okay.
Really now? Everyone's been very brave.
Go rest. Eat your fill, then sleep tight.
Eh... We... don't have much food... My portion's... not very big...
Take care of yourself... too... Uchitel...
Vetochki really trusts you, Rankin, always calling you 'Uchitel', like you're her teacher.
Everyone trusts you. They're waiting for you to get them out of here.
I know, which is why every word I tell her is what I know to be true in my heart.
It is the same as when I lead everyone. I take the path I believe to be right.
Let's hope you are.
Relax a little, Nikto. Today is the day of our first victory. It's a good omen. No need to look so anxious.
When someone awakens to the truth and decides to set off on a journey, it is the grandest of beginnings.
Quoting from Igor's biography again?
No, those are my own words.
Trust me, Nikto. We are doing great things right now.
All the surgeries are complete, Captain.
Some of the patients are developing fevers, and we're limited in what we can do...
Got it. I'll go have a word with their leader after this, and do my best to ensure the wounded are provided with food and water.
You've worked hard today. Get some rest. Taking care of yourselves is important too.
Captain, we still need to—
The Far North is under martial law, and the weather is affecting our ability to communicate too. It's difficult for us to connect to the outside.
What happened here will surely draw the attention of the Ursine authorities. We need to leave ASAP. We can't stay here.
But do we really need to work with them?
I'm worried that if we do, we'll be drawn into a conflict with Ursus...
Rhodes Island has no reason to make an enemy of the Empire, but right here, right now, these are just people struggling to survive.
Changing the lives of the Infected has always been our mission.
Besides, they have also helped us out a lot.
Don't worry for now, just do your best to prepare for the unexpected. I'll keep trying to get in contact with our base in the city.
If they can receive the information we sent earlier... I hope they will notice the clues we left behind when they come to help us.

lines.loc: Bresk, Far North, UrsusThree days ago

lines.x: Bresk Times, get your copy here—
Do you want one, devushka?
Oh, s-sure. How much?
Twenty para, is that okay?
That's fine.
But I don't have any spare change on me... Here, take this.
Thank you, devushka! You're amazing!
Amiya carefully places an entire chervonets in the boy's palm. He quickly disappears around the street corner, as if frightened his benefactor might regret her decision and take it back.

opts: Amiya?
What're you looking at?

lines.x: That boy's clothes looked... very thin.
Bresk is colder than the Ursus I remember.

opts: It was winter the last time we were in Ursus.
Bresk is further north than Chernobog.

lines.x: I still remember...
It was the first time I saw such intense fighting. I was so nervous I forgot to be scared.
At the time, I thought I would never see anything so dangerous again.

opts: We've been through a lot since then.
At least now there's no Catastrophes or people chasing us.
At least now I know who I actually am.

lines.x: Yes.
On that topic, Doctor, it's rather rare for us to have the chance to quietly watch a city like this, isn't it?
Chernobog, Lungmen, Londinium... Wherever we go, our memories seem to be filled with chaos and the fires of war.

opts: It's how this land should be.
We should remember what this city is like now.
Let's check today's paper.
Does it say why the mines have been blocked off?

lines.x: (Shakes head)
We might only be able to learn more once Eloysius has finished negotiating with the Grand Duchess.
She said she would help us apply for a permit to enter the mining district, but how will she actually go about doing that?

opts: She made sure we knew how limited our info is.
She wants us on the back foot.

lines.x: The Grand Duchess's office is right ahead.
From the intelligence we gathered beforehand, her name is Elisaveta Konstantinovna Lacitsara.
She's not the typical Ursine noble. Her policies are known for being kind, to Infected and non-Infected alike.
Every channel of information we've checked describes her as a good person.

opts: A woman like that deserves more than just 'kindness'.
The Grand Duchess must also be a bright woman.
Her office is rather humble-looking.
Almost looks like a regular home.

lines.x: The buildings in Bresk are relatively short and regular. It must be the style here in the Far North.
But looking at the map, all the surrounding buildings seem to almost envelop it. A rather unique location, in terms of urban planning.

opts: So, here we are again.
They're not even letting us wait inside this time.
A no-nonsense young lady.
A local leader with quite the reputation.

lines.x: Are you worried, Doctor?
Honestly speaking, so am I. Even if Eloysius has made her thoughts on the matter clear, we still do not know her true goal—

opts: (Shakes head)
Doesn't this all feel rather familiar?

lines.x: Oh... You mean like in Lungmen?
Ch'en really is no-nonsense, isn't she?

opts: Don't you mean 'hard to work with'?

lines.x: Let's go with 'strict'.
Ch'en treats others the same way she treats herself. That's how it was back then, that's how it is now.
Oh, right! We got news from her before we left home. While she didn't go into detail on her movements, she sounded well.
When she heard about what happened, she promised that if Rhodes Island needed her, she would return.

opts: We've made a lot of friends.
But a lot have left us too.

lines.x: I still remember when we were working with Ch'en, the one who actually represented Rhodes Island and negotiated with Chief Wei was Dr. Kal'tsit.
Doctor, I'm worried that I won't be able to solve problems or get people to work with us like she did.
Also, we know practically nothing about Ursus's current circumstances, or the Grand Duchess.
Doctor, I...

opts: We don't need to do what only Kal'tsit could do.
Be yourself, Amiya.
We'll find a way.

lines.x: May I ask which of you is Dr. Doctor of Rhodes Island?
Her Imperial Highness the Grand Duchess invites you to meet with her.
We thank our honored guest for your patience. Please follow me.
You comply with the guard's request, patiently waiting again in the office lobby for Eloysius and the much-rumored Grand Duchess's discussion to come to a close.
The subdued interior has no clock or other timekeeping instrument, as if time is not a right conferred upon its visitors.
You while away the minutes in utter boredom, thinking back to your own discussion not long ago—
Only the Doctor is allowed to meet with her?
I also think that's unreasonable. If something unexpected happens, there's no way for Amiya and I to protect you.

opts: In the end, this is her domain.
It does not seem like she is giving us any room to bargain.

lines.x: Eloysius said she wanted to introduce us to the Grand Duchess, but we still don't know why she really wants to help us.
My memory bank has no record of a young Ursine noble like her either.
But a woman acting as a special ambassador, who also personally knows the Grand Duchess, must be extraordinary.

opts: She has many secrets.
This relationship between a Grypherburger envoy and a local noble is a subtle thing.
But she did clearly say she wanted us to go with her to the mining district.
We need to cooperate with her, no matter what.
This is the only chance we have to get into the mining district.

lines.x: But Rhodes Island has stepped into the sights of Ursus, and we have no way of knowing how the local administration views us.

opts: I've had my fair share of past experience.
I've been in this sort of situation before.

lines.x: I will support your decisions, Doctor, but please be careful.
Take a comms terminal, at least! If anything happens, reach out immediately. We'll come get you right away, even if we have to smash the place up while we're at it!

opts: I will if I have to.
I'll make sure I don't need to use it.
But...

lines.x: What is this mine lockdown hiding...?
The corridors of the building are exceptionally empty, just like the streets of the city it stands in.
The moment you are led inside, a slight nod is all you are left with. Not a word has passed your guide's lips the entire time.
Perhaps he trusts your intuition will lead you to the correct room, or perhaps he is used to putting visitors to the test.
Fortunately, you had detected the warm scent of applewood smoke wafting off that guard, which takes you to the only door emitting such a smell.
A door that leads to a room tucked away in the corner was once pushed open.
It is as if the looming, imposing stone walls of this chill-gloomed city have come together to hide a spot of sacrosanct tranquility.
The first thing you see is a graceful, ash-blue figure standing at the other side of the room.

opts: Greetings.
Your Imperial Highness, the Grand Duchess Elisaveta Konstantinovna Lacitsara.

lines.x: I can feel your nerves from here. You need not be wary. Please, call me Grand Duchess Elisabeth, as my friends do.
I can tell that you are an individual of keen intellect.
You are to address Her Imperial Highness as Grand Duchess Elisabeth.
Forgive me, Your Imperial Highness. I am obligated to maintain protocol and decorum.
What does it matter?
True goodwill has nothing to do with formality. Allow our guest to relax a little.
Her voice is gentle and innocent, not at all what you expected.
The mistress of the place slowly turns around.
I welcome each and every guest to Bresk with open arms.
Is this not the part where you introduce your friend, Elotta?
Oh, yes, of course. This is the one I was just speaking about, the Doctor of Rhodes Island Pharmaceuticals—

opts: My name is Doctor.
You may call me Dr. Doctor.
It is an honor to be granted an audience.
I hope I am not too bold in paying you a visit.

lines.x: As I said, there is no need for such formality. Come forward, closer to the fire. My attendant shall fetch us some tea forthwith.
It may be difficult for travelers from afar to adapt to Bresk in this season. Though she may be remote, there are times when she is a warm and pleasant home.
Has your journey here been a smooth one?

opts: I fear I can't describe it as such...
Public Security certainly cares for Bresk.

lines.x: Please believe me when I say that all you have seen here is in service to maintaining the city's hard-fought serenity.
Elotta has already explained to me in great detail the excellent work her new Oripathy specialist friends have done in the field. Yours is a talent our city sorely needs at this moment.

opts: You require Oripathy specialists?
Ursus... requires Oripathy specialists?

lines.x: Our young Tsar wishes to bestow his favor equally upon every Ursine citizen. He even wishes for the division of infection to no longer plague his people.
It is for this reason that he labors himself to exhaustion, such that even the most steely-eyed courtier is left heartbroken at the sight.
But rare is the one who truly understands how precious a treasure His Imperial Majesty's innocence is. We who call ourselves his followers must do all we can to enact his will.

Bresk borders the largest mining district in the Far North, but the methods we have available to treat Oripathy fall far short of His Imperial Majesty's ideals. Which means we require some able doctors.
If that is Your Imperial Highness's goal, Dr. Doctor is the best partner you will find here.

I have read Rhodes Island's scientific papers for myself. Unlike those dusty old scholars of the Imperial Universities who simply repeat the findings of others, Rhodes Island leads the vanguard in the field of Oripathy research.
Oh my... I have never heard our proud young officer express such admiration for anyone before.
Has working in Grypherburg changed you? I still distinctly remember your father grumbling to me about the awkward situations you would provoke at the ducal banquets.
A rather embarrassing part of my past, Your Imperial Highness.
If I may explain this admiration further, I have always respected those with true talent and ability, such as Your Imperial Highness, and Dr. Doctor.
I am incredibly grateful for the reminder about my father. I nearly forgot to pass on his regards.
His factories still produce uniforms of the highest quality for every soldier in the Far North. His name can still be heard at every banquet on the frontier.
We had best return to the topic of our friend here.
Let us speak plainly. What plans does Rhodes Island have for Bresk?

opts: We hope you will allow us to enter the Far Northern mining district.
We lost contact with a mobile medical team in the Central Mining District.

lines.x: Upon hearing your words, the Grand Duchess shows a split-second of surprise, but does not forget herself.
She looks toward Eloysius, and you notice the precise moment the young Guardswoman's gaze turns evasive.
The Grand Duchess lets out a long sigh, as if she were vexed by a terrible burden.
Oh dear, oh dear... Dr. Doctor—that is how I should address you, yes?
If this young lady has provoked a misunderstanding, then I do apologize.
We welcome Rhodes Island building an office in Bresk, and I wish to offer as much aid as I can...
But the Far Northern mines are not open to sightseers at the moment.
If your comrades are lost, then I ask you to leave with me their descriptions and last known whereabouts. I shall deploy every last man I can spare to help you find them.

opts: Pardon my candor, but I believe we need to discuss this further.

lines.x: I am afraid—
Your Imperial Highness.
What if I invite them to travel with me, under my name?
Oh, Elotta... You always were such a naughty little rascal.
You know that Bresk needs specialist Oripathy doctors.
You know that with your recommendation, I would not consider this a favor to be repaid, allowing your friend to do business here.
Now look here. What is it you wish to do? Drag our esteemed guest into our muddy, snow-laden troubles?
Elotta... You are complicating the situation.
This is not a complicated matter. Pray forgive my bluntness, Your Imperial Highness.
I have never concealed my objective here.
By the Tsar, there is hardly a soul in Grypherburg concerned about the possibility of unrest in your Bresk.
But there are many curious about the closure of the mining district, and the mutations among the Infected.
Those coming here may be idle young nobles, or scientists researching Oripathy, but they can just as easily be an Army captain, or dignitaries from the State Council.
The young Guardswoman's tone remains frivolous, leaving you barely able to discern if the sharpness she speaks with is one of irreverent rudeness, or if there is a prying, deeper meaning to it.
Grand Duchess Elisabeth's eyes narrow.
If they have their suspicions, then let them come and speak those suspicions here.

The Armies see me as a fence-sitter currying favor with the new nobles, while the State Council sees me as some provincial Grand Duchess. I am well used to everyone laying their doubts and curiosities onto Bresk.
So, my dear Elotta, who sent you here to heap more blame upon me?
No, no, no! Your Imperial Highness misunderstands!
You know that this is just a cushy job for me, paying a visit to your demesne.

My only intention was to petition you to grant these Oripathy specialists permission to accompany me into the mining district, that we might investigate the strange happenings surrounding the Infected there.

opts: May I butt in with some words of my own?

lines.x: Pray, speak, my friend.

opts: I would like to clarify Rhodes Island's position.
We are simply a pharmaceutical company trying to address Infected issues.
We are worried for the safety of our lost friends, and must find them as soon as possible.
As for other matters, Rhodes Island will not, and cannot, get involved.

lines.x: I did not ask this earlier, but my curiosity abounds.
Your colleagues, whyever did they go to the Central Mining District?

opts: They went to address cases of Oripathy.
They are Ursine themselves.

lines.x: Oh...

opts: Rhodes Island's intentions are pure.
We have no higher power pulling our strings, after all.
We know barely anything about this place, after all.

lines.x: The moment your words leave your mouth, you suddenly realize that being overly frank could make your promises seem hollow, but this sort of overt honesty also makes it hard for others to believe you are hiding anything.
The Grand Duchess ponders for a moment.
The Grand Duchess, who has been listening intently to you the entire time, finally seems to soften.
She has full control over the expression on her face, but you sense her relax a little.

opts: In short, everything we do is in the service of searching for our friends.
This is our only request.

lines.x: A young woman I care for, and healers of Oripathy who only wish to find their comrades...
If I were to refuse you, I would seem rather cruel, wouldn't I?
I understand your position. You were just worried for our friends from afar, worried that they would get drawn into danger.
Naturally, I do not wish anyone in my lands to meet with misfortune. If I ever have the time, I would like to send stoves out to every soul who lives upon the frozen earth.
Elotta, can you promise me you will ensure our friends' safety, to the best of your ability?
Your Imperial Highness may trust in me.
Good, good. Go now, Dr. Doctor. Go find your people.
I permit Rhodes Island to enter the mining district of the Far North.

opts: Thank you for your kindness.

lines.x: I wish that I were so kind, but few are they who appreciate the kindnesses of others.
People revere those who snatch their rights from their hands, and are grateful whenever a portion of those rights is returned to them. But I do not believe such acts can be considered kind.
The winds of the Far North are bitter indeed. You had best pack some warm clothes, Dr. Doctor.
Doctor, Eloysius! You're back!
Indeed. No need to look so excited, Gospozha Amiya.
This is the administrative center of Bresk, not some den of evil. Our chat went rather smoothly, da?

opts: As smooth as silk.
Though with some unexpected developments.
You're still hiding things.
Both from us, and the Grand Duchess.

lines.x: Alright, yes, I admit that.
But it really was the only way I could create the ideal conditions for discussion. And the results speak for themselves, no?

opts: The Grand Duchess...

lines.x: What of her?

opts: She isn't what I imagined.

lines.x: Oh? And how did you imagine her?

opts: Colder.
Harder to persuade.

lines.x: If you were to only look at how she handled these strange happenings, then you would not be wrong.

After they occurred, the affected Infected were immediately sent to the mining district's research institute, where she tasked the researchers to find the cause within a week, upon pain of exile or deportation.

Soon after that, she declared martial law in the Far North, sealing all the miners within the district and blocking all communications, leaving the outside completely blind to what was happening inside.
Highly efficient, and colder than winter.
But so long as you can pinpoint exactly what she is trying to conceal from you, it is not difficult to persuade her.
The problem is that most of the time, she will leave you no opportunity to find that weak point.
Those Infected, for example. Are they affected by changes in pathology, some new symptoms of Oripathy, or something else?

That's where the issue lies, the lack of details. That is all the information that made it to Grypherburg. The moment the Infected change, they are sent off to be supervised by the research institute.
I have read your paper on the changes in Originium and new Oripathy symptoms, as have research institutes across Ursus, who are already launching their own projects based on that paper.
If these changes were simply those mentioned within, they would not merit this sort of response.
It is highly likely that the abnormalities springing up in the mining district are an entirely new threat.
An entirely new threat...
The institute looking into the cause, the district under lockdown, all the miners isolated from the wider country...
This way, everyone will believe the matter is settled, but in truth?

After the incident, all work in the district stopped, and all communications were cut off. Deity Grypherburg's inquiries have received no response. None is able, and none dares, to look too deeply into this.

opts: If the Grand Duchess wanted to hide things...
Then we would never have been allowed into Bresk.

lines.x: You have met her, and perhaps this has you believing she truly is a 'kind' administrator.
Of course she would have let you in. After all, this is 'the friendliest place to the Infected in Ursus', 'the Pearl of the Far North', 'Paradise in North Ursus'—
Bresk, capital of Breskenoy.
You didn't see anything unusual in the city these past two days, aside from the lack of people. That is how she runs the Far North.
Like how, since she became governor, nobody has been sentenced to death in the governorate. Has that lessened the number of people here who have met tragic ends? No.

opts: You seem to have a lot of complaints about her.

lines.x: Oh, you wound me. I promise my assessment just now was not tinged by any personal feelings. I was simply answering your questions.
We are partners now, aren't we?
I understand, Eloysius. Are these abnormal Infected still under the supervision of the research institute in the mining district?
Indeed. To be more precise, they are in the Central Far North Mining District Laboratory.

opts: Isn't that...?

lines.x: That's the first place we need to visit.
Is it now? Having the same destination would make things much more convenient.
Anything else you would like to ask?

opts: You're very straightforward.
You're very eager.

lines.x: Of course. I never intended to give you the run-around in the first place.
I do have a question, Eloysius.
Ask away.
Earlier, when we were at your friend's house, we had to cut our conversation short. I should have asked you this question first, but I never found the opportunity.
What is it you hope to gain from this investigation?
...If I may be serious for a moment?
I want the truth, the truth about the mutations in the Central Mining District's Infected.
I do not want the Infected miners to suffer unduly, nor do I want the people of Ursus to sit ignorant of a looming disaster.

lines.loc: Central Far North Mining District, Ursus

lines.x: We're very close to the institute.
But it's been so quiet the entire way... Do people really live here?
Three days ago, the Grand Duchess made a sudden decree that all residents living within thirty klicks of the Central Mining District had twenty-four hours to evacuate the area.
Though the Far North is sparsely populated, evacuating such a large area in such a short time was no easy task.
Not to mention ensuring they all maintain secrecy the entire time...
There are rumors that the Grand Duchess has always maintained absolute control over all affairs in this region of Ursus. Some even say she is revered even more than...
Gosudar-batyushka, I should not be running my mouth like this.

opts: She eventually allowed us in...
Which means she is not worried about our investigation.

lines.x: That is not necessarily the case.
She can hardly show resistance to a Grypherburger Imperial Guardswoman's face, can she?

Objectively speaking, the Grand Duchess has been acting as lubricant between the Armies and the State Council, making sure the bitterness between the two sides does not devolve into outright conflict.
I must admit, this requires considerable skill.
She gets the favor of both sides, but this also means she must maintain absolute impartiality.

opts: You don't seem averse to discussing this.
I thought you couldn't speak lightly on this topic in Ursus.

lines.x: Haha... Surprised? You seem to have some misunderstandings about Ursus, Dr. Doctor.
I am simply repeating the sort of gossip you might hear at any Grypherburg banquet.
If you do not know these rumors inside and out, how would you manage to join these dinnertime discussions?
Naturally, idle chatter is one thing, actually trying to leverage this chatter for some upward mobility and putting it into action is another thing altogether.

opts: A rather interesting point of view.

lines.x: On the other hand, regardless of whether the Grand Duchess is hiding anything, or if she believes everything hidden well enough...
We will certainly find something in the research institute looking into the abnormal Infected.
Do you hear... the sound of fighting up ahead?
Are those... miners fighting with a Patrol Unit?

opts: We can't just sit by and watch.
All hands, prepare a rescue operation.

lines.x: W-Wait a sec!
I've got sharper eyes, a clearer view!
The ones getting beaten down... are the Patrol Unit?
...What?
Off in the distance where Elysium is pointing, you see two groups of people fighting in a melee, a melee with a few surprising aspects to it.
A Patrol Unit is being routed by a group of Infected miners who outnumber them several times over.
The lackluster equipment in the Patrol Unit's hands is of no use, as the miners' wood-hafted pickaxes strike their bodies over and over.
Umm...
Doctor, are we... still going to mount a rescue here?

opts: We need to stop things from escalating.
We need to learn more about what's going on.

lines.x: Enough! Stop! Stop it!
What d'you think you're trying to do?! Kill us?! Blyat, Ursus will never let you get away with it!
*pant*... *pant*...

So what... So what? Since when have we ever been treated as human beings? We're people too, aren't we...?
We were never going to make it far anyway... might as well take you with us...
Just die already!
My hand—
Wh-Where did you come from?! You monster!
Monster? That's my name, don't wear it out.
Sorry, mister, but could you slow down a bit? We have some questions we need to ask you.
Why? You want to protect these Patrol Unit bastards? You're here to take us in too?
Never, mudak! We're not going to follow you back all nice and quiet!
The angry miner gets even more agitated, lifting the pickaxe in his hands up high.

lines.st: 'Stop.'

lines.x: A voice out of nowhere floods into his brain, making the weapon in his hands feel extraordinarily heavy, and his legs feel full of lead. Try as he might, he cannot move.
What the—Chyort poberi! What kind of Arts is this?
Sir... Could you allow us the chance to talk things through?
Amiya steps forward, stretching her right arm out towards the miners, exposing the Originium crystals on her wrist that were hidden under her sleeve.
As you can see, I am Infected. Most of my team are too.
We mean you no harm. Please believe me.
Infected... You're Infected like us.
And with some amazing Arts, too...
You... You must be those 'Reunion' guys!
Whuh?
No. We are not Reunion.
We are from Rhodes Island, a pharmaceutical company.
Oh... My mistake. I thought you were the legendary Reunion Movement.
I knew it... A bunch of Infected coming together to form an organization? It's a fantasy, no matter how you look at it.

opts: May I ask...?
Where did you hear the name Reunion?
What have you heard about this Reunion?

lines.x: Rankin. It was Rankin who told us about it.

To the south, real close to the border with Kazimierz, there's a group of Infected who founded a place of their own, who take in all Infected who ask for help, and drive out anyone who wants to do harm to the Infected too.
Rankin would never lie to anyone, and I really hope it's true, too! But... Kazimierz is so far away. How are we supposed to get there?
This Rankin, is he a member of Reunion?
Maybe? I'm not too sure, to be honest.
But he's our hero. When he's around, the Patrol Units won't dare mess with us. I heard he rescued those guys who got dragged into the research institute—
Hey, blabbermouth! We still don't know what these people want!
Oh... Right.
So, why're you here?
We lost contact with our friends in the area.
Have you seen a mobile medical team? There are nine of them, wearing clothes with the same logo as ours, and they would have been handing out medicine here.
Handing out medicine... What, like Santa Claus in the fairy tales?
The miners look at each other, as if they have just heard the worst joke on Terra.

opts: So, what was going on here earlier?
(Point at the cowering Patrol Unit)

lines.x: Poshel nahuy! They are murderers who wanted to kill us! They locked us up and gave us no food, trying to starve us to death!

It must have been because we couldn't dig up anything, so they didn't give us a single bite to eat, right? Stupid bastards! Who the hell even knows what happened to the vein, the Originium just disappeared all of a sudden!
We couldn't take it anymore! We had to run, or we would all die!
Is this true?
Wh-Why are you looking at me like that? What're you going to do to me?
The mining district has had a supply shortage for a while now, more than just a couple of days! We got given about, what, a few loaves of bread this month? We want to know what's going on too!
